_notes: Approximate infrared band centers (microns) for molecules relevant to exoplanet atmosphere characterization with JWST. Widths are rough half-widths for first-pass matching. This table is a convenience default for offline use. For rigorous line-by-line identification, replace with a real HITRAN line list via hitran_classifier.hitran_loader and re-run with --hitran-file.

molecules.name: Water vapor
molecules.significance: Common in both habitable and uninhabitable atmospheres. Necessary but not sufficient context for habitability.
molecules.reference: Common atmospheric molecule; not itself a biosignature.

molecules.name: Carbon dioxide
molecules.significance: Ubiquitous in rocky planet atmospheres; context molecule, not a biosignature on its own.

molecules.name: Methane
molecules.significance: Can be biogenic or abiotic (volcanism, hydrothermal chemistry). Biosignature strength depends heavily on co-occurrence with other gases, especially with oxidizing species like O2 or O3 (disequilibrium pairs).
molecules.reference: Requires context; classic disequilibrium-pair biosignature candidate when found with O2/O3.

molecules.name: Molecular oxygen
molecules.significance: Difficult to sustain abiotically at high abundance on an Earth-like rocky planet over geologic time. One of the strongest classical biosignature candidates, especially paired with reduced gases like CH4.
molecules.reference: High-priority biosignature target; false positives possible via photolytic runaway processes on some planet types.

molecules.name: Ozone
molecules.significance: Photochemical byproduct of O2; used as an indirect O2/biosignature proxy when O2 bands are too weak to detect directly.
molecules.reference: Indirect oxygen proxy; strong biosignature candidate in disequilibrium context.

molecules.name: Nitrous oxide
molecules.significance: Primarily biogenic on Earth (microbial nitrogen cycle); abiotic production pathways are limited. Considered an underrated biosignature candidate.
molecules.reference: Biogenic on Earth; low false-positive potential relative to abundance needed.

molecules.name: Dimethyl sulfide
molecules.significance: Produced by marine phytoplankton on Earth; no known efficient abiotic production pathway in planetary atmospheres. High-value, low-false-positive biosignature candidate if confirmed.
molecules.reference: Notable in K2-18 b JWST discussion; requires careful confirmation due to spectral confusion with other species.

molecules.name: Methyl chloride
molecules.significance: Biogenic on Earth (produced by marine organisms, biomass burning); minor abiotic pathways exist. Considered a secondary biosignature candidate.
molecules.reference: Secondary biosignature candidate; volcanic and biological sources both possible.

molecules.name: Ammonia
molecules.significance: Its absence, paradoxically, has been used as an indirect argument for an ocean scrubbing it out on sub-Neptune worlds. Not a direct biosignature.
molecules.reference: Context molecule; relevant to interior-atmosphere interaction arguments.

molecules.name: Carbon monoxide
molecules.significance: Its depletion relative to CO2 is sometimes cited as circumstantial biological evidence, but CO itself is not a biosignature.
molecules.reference: Context molecule for disequilibrium chemistry arguments.

molecules.name: Hydrogen sulfide
molecules.significance: Volcanically and biologically produced on Earth; ambiguous on its own without additional context.
